title: Choose metrics that match the real objective
description: Pick evaluation metrics that reflect the decision the model drives, especially under class imbalance, rather than defaulting to accuracy.
body: Use when defining how success is measured. Do not report accuracy on an imbalanced problem — a model predicting the majority class scores high while being useless. Actions: identify the costly error type, choose precision/recall/F1, ROC-AUC, or a cost-weighted metric accordingly, and report the confusion matrix alongside the headline number. Validation signals include a metric that moves when the model's useful behavior changes and per-class breakdowns that expose minority-class failure. Risk boundary: optimizing the wrong metric ships a model that looks good and harms the actual decision; confirm the metric with the stakeholder before tuning.
